title: Drip Review 2025: Features, Pricing & Honest Verdict
meta_description: Is Drip worth it in 2025? We break down pricing, features, pros and cons so you can decide.
h1: Drip Review 2025: Everything You Need to Know
intro: Drip is ecommerce email marketing made easy that positions itself for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. Founded in 2013, it has become one of the most recognised platforms in the email marketing space. In this review we cover what it does well, where it falls short, and who should actually use it.
verdict: Drip is a solid choice for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. Its paid-only pricing reflects its focus on serious senders. The main trade-off is between its particular strengths and the limitations that make alternatives worth considering for some users.
pros: Good fit for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration
cons: Can get expensive at higher subscriber counts
Some features locked to higher tiers
pricing_summary: Drip starts at $39/month, with paid plans from $39/month. Pricing typically scales with subscriber count.
best_for_detail: Drip works best for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. If you fit that profile, the feature set aligns well. If you need something different, check the alternatives section.

faq.a: Drip does not offer a free plan. Paid plans start at $39/month. This makes it more suited to established senders with a budget.

faq.q: What is Drip best used for?
faq.a: Drip is best for e-commerce businesses wanting deep store integration. Its standout features include e-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, visual automation builder, which suit this use case well.
Drip is aimed at users with some email marketing experience. The interface is straightforward for basic campaigns, though advanced automation features have a learning curve.

faq.q: Does Drip have an affiliate program?
faq.a: Yes, Drip runs an affiliate program paying 15% recurring. This is worth considering if you plan to recommend the platform to your audience.
